mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate hike periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising industrial production signals resilient corporate output and pricing power, overriding rate headwinds to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods correlate with negative 5-day returns in greater than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, consistently generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance at p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 confirm robust economic momentum and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Months with payrolls above 200k show negative 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real personal consumption expenditures grow faster than real GDP indicates consumption-led economic expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth outpacing GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.5%, ultra-loose monetary conditions provide immediate liquidity support and lower discount rates, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%.
